findings: Passed. The plate depicts an adult male Red-bellied Woodpecker: one head, one straight gray-black bill, two wings, two legs with climbing feet, and one stiff pointed tail are represented without visible anatomical duplication.

Species identification is supported by the red crown and nape, pale buff-gray face and underparts, narrow black-and-white barring on the back and wings, subtle pink-red lower belly, and the trunk-clinging pose. These agree with the supplied field-reference photos and both live sources.

All visible factual text is accurate: Melanerpes carolinus, Picidae, length 9.4 in (24 cm), wingspan 13.0–16.5 in (33–42 cm), weight 2.0–3.2 oz (56–91 g), habitat, behavior, sex-specific head pattern, flight wing flashes, and food caching are supported.
No place name, ZIP code, coordinates, map, or local-observation detail appears on the candidate plate.

verification_sources.title: Red-bellied Woodpecker Identification — All About Birds
